demo_name: Fred Hutch OMOP Ingestion — Data Engineering Session

deploy_target.note: No data-generation job. This kit READS the 6 OMOP source tables from source_schema (read-only) and WRITES bronze/recon/config tables into client_schema. The source tables must already exist. They are produced by the foundation setup job (the `foundation/` bundle, `foundation_setup_job`).

toggle.note: There is no run_with_synthetic_data boolean here. Synthetic vs real = which schema source_schema points at. Synthetic = clinops_foundation (shared foundation). Real = the FH OMOP schema (e.g. curated_omop.omop). The 6 OMOP table names are identical either way, so no query changes are required to switch.

tables.name: person
tables.role: Read-only OMOP source, one row per patient.

tables.name: condition_occurrence
tables.role: Read-only OMOP source. In nb 01 a v2 append adds condition_source_name to demonstrate schema evolution.

tables.name: measurement
tables.role: Read-only OMOP source. In nb 02 a copy has a deliberate 7-row gap injected to exercise reconciliation.

tables.name: bronze_*
tables.role: Bronze landing tables the TEAM creates in client_schema (e.g. bronze_person). nb 01 evolves bronze on append via mergeSchema.

tables.name: recon_summary
tables.role: Timestamped reconciliation audit table the TEAM writes in nb 02 (one row per table per run).

tables.name: ingest_allowlist
tables.role: UC config table (6 OMOP allowed, 2 restricted denied) the restricted-ingest gate reads in nb 03.

bronze.note: Bronze landing written by the ingest notebooks into client_schema.

silver.note: This DE track owns ingest hardening plus the trials catalog. It flattens the Volume-fed trials feed to silver_trial_criteria, which is the eligibility contract the Applied AI pre-screen joins against. Both groups build in parallel off the 6 OMOP tables; neither waits on the other's data layer. See ../../SHARED_FOUNDATION.md.

dependency_map.note: Read-only OMOP source → bronze in client_schema
nb 02 compares source vs target counts + anti-join for missing keys
nb 03 reads the allow-list before any write
The trials catalog silver_trial_criteria is the eligibility contract the Applied AI pre-screen joins against (a hand-off, never a blocker)

source_inputs.description: The 6 read-only OMOP source tables. Synthetic default = clinops_foundation.
source_inputs.active_when: always (this kit does not generate data)

source_inputs.description: FH real OMOP CDM tables — point source_schema here to run against real data. Identical table names.

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 0 — all 6 read-only OMOP source tables present with expected counts
verify_queries.sql: SELECT 'person' AS tbl, COUNT(*) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${source_schema}.person UNION ALL SELECT 'condition_occurrence', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${source_schema}.condition_occurrence UNION ALL SELECT 'measurement', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${source_schema}.measurement UNION ALL SELECT 'observation', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${source_schema}.observation UNION ALL SELECT 'drug_exposure', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${source_schema}.drug_exposure UNION ALL SELECT 'note', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${source_schema}.note ORDER BY tbl
verify_queries.expected: person=300, condition_occurrence=300, measurement=720, observation=720, drug_exposure=383, note=265

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 1 — after the mergeSchema append, bronze has condition_source_name and 600 rows (300 NULL pre-evolution + 300 populated)
verify_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n, COUNT(condition_source_name) AS populated FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.bronze_condition_occurrence

verify_queries.description: Checkpoint 2 — reconciliation flags the injected 7-row measurement gap
verify_queries.sql: SELECT table_name, status, delta FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.recon_summary ORDER BY event_time DESC
verify_queries.expected: measurement FAIL delta=7; all other tables PASS

lock_targets.reason: OMOP CDM source table names (person, condition_occurrence, measurement, observation, drug_exposure, note) are standard. Renaming breaks the read from source_schema and the synth→real repointing.
Adaptation skill must not modify itself

checkpoints_note: Notebook-driven checkpoints (Checkpoint 0-4) are documented in RUNBOOK.md. Some (restricted-ingest gate nb 03, SLA window nb 04) are Python assertions rather than SQL — verify by running the notebook cells, not a warehouse query.
